text.title: Frontend i18n: react-intl, RTL, Pseudo-Localisation
text.summary: react-intl ICU messages, RTL layout with CSS logical properties, and pseudo-localisation for CI truncation detection.

rule.statement: Use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start, text-align: start) instead of physical (margin-left, text-align: left); set dir="rtl" on the html element.
rule.rationale: Physical properties don't flip in RTL; logical properties do automatically. Flipping text-align: right instead of using start is a common agent mistake.

section.title: Pseudo-Localisation

rule.statement: Run pseudo-localisation in CI staging permanently to surface hardcoded strings and truncation before translators are involved.
rule.rationale: German text is ~30% longer than English; Arabic with diacritics can be 200% longer. Without pseudo-loc, truncation bugs are found only after expensive translation rounds.

section.reference: Generate test catalogue with [!!...~~] padding to surface truncation

antipattern.description: Allowing HTML inside translated strings as free-form markup.
antipattern.reason: Translators inject XSS or mangle tags. Use component interpolation with placeholders (e.g., react-intl's values prop with JSX elements) and re-render safely.
